sql_array: CREATE TABLE roles (role_id INT PRIMARY KEY, role_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE users (user_id INT PRIMARY KEY, username VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, role_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (role_id) REFERENCES roles(role_id));
INSERT INTO users (user_id, username, role_id) VALUES (1, 'alice', 1), (2, 'bob', 2), (3, 'charlie', NULL);
SELECT username, USER_ROLE_DROP(role_id) FROM users WHERE role_id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT count(*) AS total_users, USER_ROLE_DROP(roles.role_id) FROM roles JOIN users ON roles.role_id = users.role_id GROUP BY roles.role_id HAVING total_users > 0;
SELECT username FROM users WHERE role_id IS NOT NULL AND username = 'admin' AND bif:sha1(username || CAST(role_id AS VARCHAR)) IS NOT NULL ORDER BY bif:sha1(username);
SELECT bif:sha1(role_name || ' ' || username) AS hashed_user_role FROM roles LEFT JOIN users ON roles.role_id = users.role_id WHERE roles.role_name IS NOT NULL AND bif:sha1(role_name) IS NOT NULL;
DELETE FROM roles WHERE role_id = (SELECT MAX(role_id) FROM roles);
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN last_login TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NULL;
UPDATE users SET last_login = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM users WHERE role_id IS NULL);
